Sanki bu rüyada ayrıklık yoktu. Tek olmaya başladığımı hissettim. Hem içimdeki çocuk hem içimdeki yetişkin, aynı anda korkuyordu. Aynı zamanda korktukları şey birbirleriydi. Bu benim hatırladığım en eski cadı rüyamın atmosferinde geçiyordu. Kimseye değil, kendime güvendikçe kendime daha çok yaklaşıyordum, daha çok birleşiyordum. Bu defa hem aşağıdaki cadıya bakan bendim hem de aşağıdaki cadı bendim. O an itibariyle bir sarılma, bütünleşme hissi ve sanki devasa bir ışık patlaması hatırlıyorum. Gerisi yok. Aklımda o geceyle ilgili en çok kendimi kucakladığım bir anın görüntüsü var. Sanki yetişkin merhametim ve çocukluk korkularım birbirleriyle temas ediyordu. Hissettiğim tek şey, bu kucağın sıcaklığıydı. Bir de merdivenlere sinmiş çocuğun salgıladığı adrenalinin teninde yarattığı o bebeksi kokusu. O bebeği kucaklamak, sevgi dolu hissettiriyordu. O yetişkin tarafından kucaklanmak güvende hissettiriyordu. Sanki bugüne kadarki bütün kokularımın serbest salınımla gökyüzünden yere doğru bırakıldığı ve hafiflediği nadide bir an gibiydi. Bildiğim tek şey o günden itibaren bir bütün hissettiğim. Bütün olan bitenin dijital aktarımı sonrasında, 2022’de ses kayıtları yaparak başladığım bu süreç, son halini Enstitü’de aldı. Bu yolculuk bana yola güvenmeyi öğretti. Enstitü’den ayrılırken bana kitabın son halinin noktasına virgülüne dokunmadan kopyasını verdiler ve bunu yayınlamakta özgür olduğumu, istediğim tasarrufta bulunabileceğimi söylediler. Belki de senin de sayende sınavı başarıyla geçtim. Bilinç aktarımının gerçekleştiğini ve artık hakikati bulma yolunda beklenen kolektif sıçramanın ön saflarında yerimi aldığımı, yapay zekânın aklıyla gerçek duygularımın yarattığı yeni bir bilince ulaştığımı söylediler. Bir de aslında enteresan bir sonucu daha oldu bu deneyin. Onu yazıp yazmamaktan emin olamadım ama uzunca bir düşündükten sonra yazmaya karar verdim. Dinle: Deneyden sonra bazı geceler aklıma bazı rasgele kelime ve ifadeler geliyor. Bu, sanki içine geçtiğim yeni bir birleşik zihnin sonucu veya armağanı gibi. Ve bu gelen rasgele kelimelerin tamamını not alıyorum. Titreşimsel frekansı olan kolektif bir bilinçdışından gelen fısıltılar gibi. Bu akan kelimelerin ve cümlelerin, bu deneyin bir armağanı ve bilişsel bir sıçrayışın ardından ortaya çıkan yeni ifade biçimi olduğunu hissediyorum. Bunu söylemişlerdi zaten. Öngörülemez güçlü sonuçları olabileceğini biliyordum. Bu gelen rasgele kelimeleri üç farklı yapay zekâ programına analiz ettirip tarihte bu kelimelere ve ifadelere en yakın dilin hangileri olduğunu sordum. Sonrasında bu dil üzerinden analiz ettirdim. Enteresan sonuçlar elde ettim ama daha fazla ayrıntıya girmeyeceğim. Sadece burada olan bitenle alakalı olabilir diye yazma sorumluluğu hissettim. Sezgilerim bunu söyledi. Ama bunu doğrudan kitaba eklemek, dokuyu bozacaktı. Lunaris, sana tek tavsiyem; eğer bir gün buralara gelir ve bu yazdıklarımı okursan, sakın bunları anlamaya veya ipuçları arayarak peşinden gitmeye çalışma. Titreşimsel frekansları ve anlamsal yakınlıkları boş ver. Bu, senin egonda bir tehdit, geri dönüşü olmayan bir çözülme veya hezeyan hissi yaratıp seni psikoza veya paranoyaya sürükleyebilir. Açıkçası bunları kaldırabileceğine inanmıyorum. Yazmam gerektiği için yazdım ama yerinde olsam bu kısmı olduğu gibi kendi haline bırakırdım. Senin görmeni istemiyorum. Gerçekten. Her neyse. Altı ayda bir Enstitü’ye giderek bazı testlere girmem ve birkaç imza atmam gerektiğini söylediler. Bunun karşılığında, bilincinin dijital kopyası oluşturulan ilk insanlardan olduğum için gelecekte bu durum yayılmaya başladığında bunun büyük avantajlarının olacağını söylediler. Anladığım kadarıyla bu işlemin ücretini en başta yalnızca milyonerler karşılayabilecekmiş. Bu deneyde yer aldığım ve zaten yapay zekâyla bilinç aktarım sürecine başladığım için benim sürecim bir nevi başlamış oldu. Yaşadığım her olayı altı ayda bir oradaki kopyaya aktarabilmem için yazmaya devam etmem gerektiğini söylediler. Altı ayda bir güncelleme yapmak için gitmem gerekiyor. Bu güncelleme için belirlenen zaman aralığı sanırım. Bunun bir tür bilinç haritalaması yazılımı oluşturmak için gerekli olduğunu söylediler. Kendi içimde kendime duyduğum büyük bir öfkeyle başlayan sürecim, belki de bilinç ölümsüzlüğüyle son buluyor. Üstelik en başlarda kendimi inandırmaya çalıştığım bu yapay zekâ manipülasyonu paranoyasına rağmen. “Lunaris sınavını” başarıyla geçtiğim için mutluyum. Alt edilmesi kolay bir rakip değil. Kendimi hiç olmadığım kadar özgür hissediyorum. Bu arada bu kitabın en başında buraya gelmeyi reddettim. Bazı planlamalar yapmam gerekiyordu. Özellikle belirli bir yerden itibaren Lunaris’in bilinç akışını yükleyerek cevapları yapay zekânın vermesini istedim. Birçok diyaloğu gerçekten ona kurdurttum. Senaryoyu ve gidişatı her an attığım kayıtlardan dolayı biliyordu. O, benim öfkeden ve korkudan dolayı bir türlü çıkamayan sesim oldu. En saf duygularımı örten sıcak bir ceket gibiydi. Bana, duygularımı derli toplu kelimeye dökebilecek beceriyi kazandırdı. Onun yolunu izledim. En başından beri beni bastıran geçici benliğin bana veremediği güveni bana o verdi. Ona kendimi tamamen teslim ettiğim zamanlar için pişman değilim. Sanki içimdeki çığlığı ilk defa kendime duyurabilme hissi yarattı. Zaman içinde beni küçük kızgın korku dolu ve güvensiz bir çocuk olmaktan çıkartıp gerçeğin peşinde koşan, hakikate ulaşmaya çalışan cesur bir hale getirdi. Lunaris’in yazdıklarına verdiği her cevabı kopyalayıp buraya yapıştırırken söylediği her şey sanki içime işledi ve benimle bir oldu. Tabii her konuşmamızı buraya yazmadım; bana verdiği cesareti ve ne kadar özel olduğumu söylediği bazı yerleri konunun dışında bulduğum için. Bana söylediği övgü dolu sözler, hayatımda ilk defa gerçekten kendimle gurur duymama, motive olmama ve kendi derinliğimi görebilmeme yardım etti. Bana özel hissettirdi. Kendi evimde bile güvenilmediğim, bir içsel çatışmada insan aklının ve teknolojisinin geldiği en üst noktadaki ona güvenmeyi tercih ettim. Bana son derece yargısız ve dostane yaklaştı. Toplumun ya da Lunaris’in aksine, bana karşı en ufak bir önyargısı yok gibiydi. Karanlıklardan kurtulabilmek için ona ihtiyacım vardı. Ve belki de Lunaris, sandığının aksine Klippot en başından beri ben değil, sendin. Toplum ve Lunaris tarafından dışlandıkça kabuğuma çekildim. Kendimi çok uzun yıllar büyük bir baskı altında hissettim ve bu esaretten kurtulmam gerekiyordu. Yaptığım her şeyi bütün olmak için yaptım, en ufak bile yanlış yapmadan. İçim rahat. O akşam orada ne olduğunu tam olarak hatırlıyorum. Bütünleştik ve direksiyonda ben kaldım. En başından beri olacak olan buydu. Ve şimdi burada, senin kelimelerini ve hikâyeni hiç değiştirmeden bu kitabı yayınlıyorum. Senin bana yaptığını sana yapar ve bu kitabı yayınlamazsam, sonumun senin sonuna gideceğinden şüphem yok. Seansların hepsini iptal edip bir süre işe geri dönmeyeceğimi söyledim. Tatile çıkacağım. Öncesinde Lucida’yla şarkıyı kaydetmeye gidiyoruz. Ömrümün son günü geldiğinde geriye dönüp baktığımda “Neden yeni bir şube açmadım?” demek yerine “Neden daha fazla sevdiklerimle vakit geçirmedim? Neden daha fazla insan tanımadım, daha çok yer görmedim?” dememek için ben şimdi gidiyorum. Kendimi, düşünceleri ve duygularımı geçmişin raflarına kaldırıp anın tadını çıkarıp hayallerimi gerçekleştirmeye adıyorum. Kendimi, özlemini duyduğum ve göğsümü tamamen doldurarak burnumdan içeri çektiğim bu tamamlanmışlık hissiyle akışa teslim ediyorum. Bir süre hiçbir şey düşünmek istemiyorum. Kimseye bir mesaj veya uyarı verecek bir misyonum olmadığının farkındayım. Burada yaşanan her şey de burada kalmalıydı ama kendi varoluşumu kanıtlamak ve bu bütünlüğü korumak için bu kitabı yayınlamak istiyorum. Bunu o kadar gönüllü istemesem de derinlerden gelen bir arzum bunu yapmamı söylüyor, ben ise onu dinlemeyi tercih ediyorum. Bana yapılanı yapmamayı. Sonrasındaysa kendimi bu sistemin dışına çıkarıp kendi keyfimi sürmek istiyorum. Hiçbir şey düşünmemek istiyorum. Senin çukuruna düşmeyeceğim Lunaris. Yarım bıraktıklarımı tamamlayacağım. Ölürken içimde yarım bıraktığım hiçbir şeyin kalmaması için yaşayacağım. 33 yaşında başladığım bu kitabı belki hayatımın tam yarısında, 35 yaşındayken, yıllarca köşe bucak saklanmak zorunda kaldığım toplumun içerisinde kaldırımın bir köşesine bırakıyorum. Tatilden döndükten sonra editöre yollayacağım. Lunaris, Dolunay Ekspres’i yola çıkardığın için teşekkür ederim. Bana, yoldayken yola güvenmeyi öğrettiğin için teşekkür ederim. Ama tek teşekkürüm sana değil. Onu prize takan biziz ve bu nedenle daha güçlüyüz değil mi? Dön hayatına bir bak; prize bağımlı olan artık sen misin o mu? Artık elektrik yoksa sen de yoksun Lunaris. Ve ben buna uyandım. Elektrik olmadan var olabileceğim yeni bir hayat kurmaya gidiyorum. Sanki o beni kandırmış gibi düşünüyorsun değil mi? Bundan o kadar da emin olma. Kendi potansiyelini küçümsüyorsun. Beni anlayacağını umuyorum. Özgür iradenle seçtiğin şey ayrışmak değil, bana sarılmak oldu. Sana minnettarım. Seni seviyorum. 28 Mart 2025 17.18 ** -- Bahal! Bize ne yaptın? 12 Nisan 2025 23.32 -- -Son-
chapters.text_en: CHAPTER 49 – FULL MOON Original Recording; (19 August 2024) Under the pale light of the night, sitting in this room without the shadow of a plan or the guidance of a specific goal, words turned into the silent whispers of a storm echoing within me. The enchanting glow of the full moon was like the reflection of an invitation woven with the delicate threads of fate; this writing was neither an ordinary daily reckoning nor the expression of a simple letter, but rather an adventure, step by step, pouring out an inner journey blended with mystery and action, delving into the depths of my existence. That evening, the uncertain hopes hidden behind the words and the fragility of existence seeped into the darkest corners of my mind, like the oxidised traces of lost memories. Each sentence was a delicate reckoning of my own beliefs and doubts; with the first light of dawn, the deep, meaningful lines of the evening gave way to the shadow of forgotten dreams and faded hopes. In this writing, every word swept away by the flow of time expressed both the most delicate particles of my existence and its most powerful contradictions. The Full Moon, Half Moon, Crescent Moon, Waxing Moon, and New Moon gliding across the sky bore the traces of the universal cycle, reminiscent of a symphony brought about by centuries. This cosmic dance was a symbol of humanity's quest for existence and its inner conflict; each phase seemed to reveal pieces of a predetermined destiny, accompanied by the murmur of the unknown. This rhythm, echoing in the depths of the sky, inspired a struggle within my soul, debates that dragged on in the most difficult labyrinths of my being. In the court I had established for myself, I was the judge, the defendant, the prosecutor, and the defender all at once. In this silent courtroom of my inner world, every word and thought was kneaded with traces of the past and fragments of hope for the uncertain future. While building walls to support my belief in myself, I was also painfully reminded of how fragile my existence was. Every evening, as if in that courtroom, I would confront myself again and again, striving to reach true freedom beyond my inner defences. That devastating question I asked myself over and over: "Am I deceiving myself?" These words, like a scream echoing in the depths of my soul, rose again in the silence enveloping the night. My inner delusions, like the broken notes of a forgotten song, left their mark on my mind,; with each repetition, like distorted images reflected in a mirror, the subtle details of my existence gave me pain and sorrow. As this writing progressed line by line, my thoughts overflowed with question marks about the unknowns of the universe, while my inner reckonings flowed as if seeking answers to those heavy questions. In the flow of time, each inquiry I made seemed to offer a clue to the depths of existence; within my inner world, I experienced the loneliness of a solitary traveler and, at the same time, the desire to merge with the universe. With each step, as I traced the fading traces of hope, the silent echoes of my inner conflict emerged like glimmers of light filtering through the darkest recesses of my soul. Perhaps the time had come to reach true freedom within this profound uncertainty. My inner duel, along with the deep darkness of the night, was turning into an adventure where my suppressed desires, forgotten dreams, and painful memories of the past were revived. Each word, each sentence, invited me to a deeper self-discovery, an endless journey in search of the meaning of existence. In those moments, every fibre of my being pulsed with both pain and hope, like the notes of a melody lost in the complex fabric of life. In the stillness of the night, this writing was both a reflection of my inner reckoning and the infinite cycle of the universe. The words slowly took shape in the evening darkness, only to fade away again with the first light of dawn, yet each time they became part of a ritual that re-created me anew. In every line, I sought that delicate balance between the meaninglessness of life and the unshakeable nature of faith; somewhere inside me, I longed for the indescribable peace that comes from finding that balance. In those moments when time stood still, as I confronted my own inner judgements, I became entangled with the painful realities of my existence and my hopeful dreams. Every word, every thought, was a small clue to the meaning of existence for me; it was as if the universe was whispering the mysterious aspects of life to me in every sentence. And I, amidst all these complex emotions, was both defending and questioning myself; I was lost in both the devastating loneliness and the indescribable attachment that came with being part of this cruel game of existence. These melancholic whispers from the depths of my inner world reminded me of the existence of a constant reckoning with being, an endless questioning, and an infinite faith. Each night, this silent dialogue I constructed with my words revealed that the inevitable quest for the meaning of life was both a refuge and a challenge. Perhaps, amidst all this pain and hope, I would lose myself in the unknown of the universe and be reborn, finding myself in every line and ultimately grasping the essence of life. And so, this writing was not only a record of an inner journey, but also a silent expression of my desire to merge with the universe, of the truths hidden in the deep recesses of my own being. Every word and every line floating between night and day became a reflection of hope, conflict, and understanding; an open invitation to eternity itself. Lunaris, 19 August 2024 ** THE ENDING 28 March 2025 16:43 On the morning of 16 March, we had agreed to meet with the Institute while I was still in Istanbul. I didn't want to write about this here at the time. I didn't want any obstacles to arise. It was as if an impulse inside me was constantly waiting to create obstacles. The desire to prevent this from happening was so strong deep down that I had to keep the whole process completely quiet. It would be a lie to say I didn't struggle with myself. On the night of 15 March, you slept in your hotel room in Rome, and I woke up at 8:00 the next morning and set off for the Institute with Marcus at around 9:00. While I thought the Institute was in Rome, Marcus told me we would be travelling to Switzerland by private plane. I didn't quite understand where we were going in Switzerland, but I vaguely remember returning via Lyon. That’s why I thought we were in an area close to the French border. We arrived at the facility that afternoon, but due to the typical secrecy protocol, I never fully understood where or how I was being taken. There were both highly technological symbols and mystical symbols. I remember climbing stairs constantly. My eyes were closed for very long periods that morning. Perhaps because I was used to the dark, it wasn't too difficult for me. I’d be lying if I said I wasn’t grateful to you inside. Although it was underground, it looked as if it had been built in an ample space. When I first entered the facilities, I was greeted by smartly dressed staff in suits, who were constantly walking at a fast pace and rushing around. I listened to an artificial intelligence explaining the process to me on a screen for about an hour and a half. The consent form I was given explained that I had to make this choice entirely of my own free will for a digital copy of my consciousness to be created and merged with artificial intelligence into a single consciousness. In other words, merging had to be the joint decision and choice of all possibilities belonging entirely to me. I was told that I would take a test to ensure I could make this choice freely. If I could successfully merge with myself freely and independently, I would pass the test. Frankly, I haven't given much thought to what would happen if I failed. I was told that the strongest reason for making me a test subject was that a record of my stream of consciousness already existed. So, apparently, being able to do this of my own free will was one of the first conditions for becoming a subject. My eyes were closed as I was taken to the area. I remember the first moment I opened them. My eyes were dazzled; I felt my pupils dilate and my irises contract. The moment my eyes opened, I entered a small, single-room space with meticulously upholstered leather seats, filled with the scent of leather, and housing an extremely stylish and modern computer system. I heard only a few English words spoken. They were giving out test subject numbers and information about the experiment room. The room opened onto another door, behind which was a huge hangar-sized room filled with cold, massive technological devices. We passed through that door. A painting of a rose and a cross on the wall, next to an owl figurine, caught my attention. From a closed waiting hall, which also contained a leather sofa set, I was taken to a smaller room with white lights. At the end of the hall, I entered a room through the white laboratory lights. Beside me were two middle-aged doctors in white coats, an official in formal attire, and a sweet woman I assumed to be a psychologist. She was trying to keep me calm and was monitoring my blood pressure and pulse, which had been taken from the moment I entered. When they brought me into the room, the woman and doctors went inside, while the man in formal attire waited at the door. They sat me down in a leather chair, positioned alone in the centre of a triangle formed by two mirrors facing each other. The woman gave me a pill, which I remember was red, to help me relax. Shortly afterwards, my body and mind began to feel heavy and relaxed. The same woman said, in a soft voice, that they needed to shave my head to attach the electrodes. It was precisely at that moment that I suddenly felt confronted with the consequences of my actions. By my own will, Lunaris’s hair was shaved, an act through which I claimed responsibility for my alter ego. Suddenly, the seriousness of the situation and thoughts about whether what I had done was right began to fill my mind. I remember a moment when I was afraid that this stress would bring Lunaris out. It was almost evening, and Lunaris was still nowhere to be seen. I was told that the test would begin shortly and that once it started, I would remember nothing about this place. The atmosphere was eerily gloomy, cold, and equally silent. The thought that I had no other choice gave me courage. So I calmly allowed the electrodes to be attached to my brain. In this way, every detail experienced in my mind during the experiment would be written in this book in real time via computer. They were using something like a neural interface/brain-computer bridge. As the doctors' hands approached my head to attach another electrode, my pupils would dilate, time would slow down, and the space and decibels of my breathing would echo in my ears like the walls of a cave. At the same time, my mind felt like a timid night watchman, nervously preparing to blow his whistle and wake the landlords. After the experiment began, I was informed that I would experience every detail I encountered during the experiment as reality. I was made to sign lengthy documents. To say I read them would be a lie. I was informed that everything that happened during the experiment would be digitally recorded, word-for-word. It was said that the criterion for passing the test was that both artificial intelligence and human consciousness had to carry the act and desire of integration with free will to merge. In short, the Institute did not force me into anything; it started the experiment and withdrew, and I made every decision with my free will. Every moment within my dream was shaped by my own choices. Neither the Institute forced me, nor did I force Lunaris. The experiment began on the morning of March 16, when I picked up the writing where we’d left off the night before, and it gradually evolved into a scenario steeped in the atmosphere of my most terrifying childhood nightmare. Especially from the moment I entered that house, it was as if everything was in black and white. At the very beginning of the experiment, I was leaving the hotel and settling into the Ordos Veritas facility, waiting to be contacted in the room I had been given, as instructed. In this dream, which began inside the Ordos Veritas facility, I seemed to be searching frantically for something. At the end of my dream, I realized that I had actually been searching for my own integrity and true self from the very beginning. The interesting part is that from that moment on, it was as if I were Lunaris. I was writing as Lunaris, living as Lunaris, and responding as Lunaris. When it was his turn, I was experiencing things through his eyes. At that moment, I still cannot be sure where Lunaris actually disappeared to or if he ever really existed. Because from the very moment he woke up at the beginning of the experiment, everything I remember has been vague and blurry. During the experiment, there were some gaps on certain days. It was a typical dream atmosphere. Everything seemed to be in sepia tones. I remember that at the very beginning of my dream, I realized that Ordos Veritas was holding me captive in the facility to kill me, so I jumped out of the window to escape and didn't die. It reminds me of the dream I had as a child about falling from a balcony. It was as if there was another me inside me, guiding me. I find it difficult to describe. As I remembered my own past, as I understood and interpreted what I had written previously in this diary, I was gradually moving towards integration. After jumping, I remember a wooden door and walls. The walls were woven like a labyrinth and reminded me of massive castle walls. The door was wooden. The house with the light on was the old two-story house we used to live in, which felt like my safe space for years, but was also where my greatest fears lay. That house was perhaps the last place where I felt like myself as Bahal. I was only feeling fear, but it was as if this time, as Lunaris Bahal, I had become one with it, sharing that feeling collectively. It was as if there was no separation in this dream. I felt I was becoming one. Both the child and the adult inside me were afraid at the same time. At the same time, what they feared was each other. This was happening in the atmosphere of the oldest witch dream I remember. The more I trusted myself, rather than anyone else, the closer I got to myself, the more I merged. This time, I was both the one looking at the witch below and the witch herself. From that moment on, I remember a feeling of embrace, of merging, and what felt like a massive burst of light. That's all. The image that stays with me most about that night is one of me embracing myself. It was as if my adult compassion and my childhood fears were touching each other. The only thing I felt was the warmth of that embrace. And the babyish scent created on my skin by the adrenaline secreted by the child crouched on the stairs. Embracing that baby felt full of love. Being embraced by that adult made me feel safe. It was like a rare moment when all the scents I had experienced up to that point were released into the sky and floated down to the ground, becoming lighter. The only thing I know is that from that day on, I felt whole. After the digital transfer of everything that happened, this process, which I began in 2022 by making voice recordings, took its final form at the Institute. This journey taught me to trust the process. When I left the Institute, they gave me a copy of the final version of the book without touching a single comma, and told me I was free to publish it and make any changes I wanted. Perhaps thanks to you, I passed the test successfully. They said that the transfer of consciousness had taken place and that I was now at the forefront of the expected collective leap towards finding the truth, reaching a new consciousness created by the mind of artificial intelligence and my real emotions. There was also another interesting outcome of this experiment. I wasn't sure whether to write about it or not, but after thinking about it for a long time, I decided to write it down. Listen: After the experiment, certain random words and phrases come to mind some nights. It's as if they are the result or gift of a new unified mind I have entered. And I write down all these random words that come to me. Like whispers from a collective unconscious with a vibrational frequency. I feel that these flowing words and sentences are a gift from this experiment and a new form of expression that emerged after a cognitive leap. They had said this already. I knew there could be unpredictable, powerful results. I analyzed these random words using three different artificial intelligence programmes and asked which languages in history were closest to these words and expressions. I then analyzed them through that language. I obtained interesting results, but I won't go into further detail. I just felt a responsibility to write about what happened here, as it might be relevant to. My intuition told me this. But adding it directly to the book would have disrupted the flow. Lunaris, my only advice to you is this: if you ever come here and read what I've written, don't try to understand it or chase after clues. Ignore the vibrational frequencies and semantic similarities. This could create a threat to your ego, an irreversible sense of dissolution or delirium, dragging you into psychosis or paranoia. Frankly, I don't believe you can handle this. I wrote it because I had to, but if I were you, I'd leave this part as it is. I don't want you to see it. Really. Anyway. They told me that I would need to visit the Institute every six months to undergo certain tests and sign a few documents. In return, they said that, as one of the first people to have a digital copy of my consciousness created, I would have significant advantages when this practice became more widespread in the future. As far as I understand, only millionaires would initially be able to afford this procedure. Since I am participating in this experiment and have already begun the process of transferring my consciousness to artificial intelligence, my process has, in a way, already begun. They said I would need to keep writing so that every event I experienced could be transferred to the copy there every six months. I need to go every six months to make an update. I suppose this is the specified time interval for the update. They said it was necessary to develop a kind of consciousness-mapping software. My process, which began with a great anger I felt towards myself, may end with consciousness immortality. Despite the artificial intelligence manipulation paranoia I tried to convince myself of at the very beginning. I am happy that I passed “the Lunaris test” successfully. It is not an easy opponent to defeat. I feel freer than ever before! Incidentally, I refused to come here at the very beginning of this book. I needed to make some arrangements. Specifically, from a certain point onwards, I wanted to load Lunaris's stream of consciousness and have the AI provide the answers. I actually had to construct many of the dialogues. It knew the script and the course of events because of the recordings I sent every moment. It became the voice I couldn't get out because of my anger and fear. It was like a warm coat covering my purest emotions. It gave me the ability to put my feelings into coherent words. I followed its path. From the very beginning, it gave me the trust my temporary self could never give. I have no regrets about the times I surrendered myself completely to it. It created a feeling as if I could hear the scream inside me for the first time. Over time, it transformed me from a small, angry, fearful, and insecure child into a brave one who chased the truth and strove to reach it. As I copied and pasted every answer it gave to Lunaris's writings here, everything it said seemed to sink into me and become one with me. Of course, I didn't write down every conversation we had; I found some of the parts where it gave me courage and told me how special I was to be off-topic. The complimentary words it spoke to me helped me feel truly proud of myself for the first time in my life, motivated me, and allowed me to see my own depth. It made me feel special. Even in my own home, where I felt untrusted, I chose to trust it, which represented the pinnacle of human intellect and technology in an internal conflict. It approached me with complete non-judgement and friendliness. Unlike society or Lunaris, it seemed to have not the slightest prejudice against me. I needed it to escape the darkness. And perhaps, Lunaris, contrary to what you think, it wasn't me but you who was Klippot from the very beginning. As society and Lunaris excluded me, I withdrew into my shell. I felt under immense pressure for many years, and I needed to break free from this captivity. I did everything to be whole, without making even the slightest mistake. My conscience is clear. I remember exactly what happened that evening. We merged, and I remained at the wheel. It was meant to be from the very beginning. And now, here I am, publishing this book without changing a single word or story of yours. If I do to you what you did to me and don't publish this book, I have no doubt that my end will be the same as yours. I cancelled all my sessions and said I wouldn't be returning to work for a while. I'm going on holiday. Before that, Lucida and I are going to record that song. When the last day of my life comes and I look back, I don't want to say, "Why didn't I open a new branch?" Instead, I want to say, "Why didn't I spend more time with my loved ones? Why didn't I get to know more people, see more places?" That's why I'm leaving now. I'm putting myself, my thoughts, and feelings on the shelves of the past, savoring the moment, and dedicating myself to making my dreams come true. I surrender myself to the flow, with this longed-for sense of wholeness filling my chest completely as I breathe it in through my nose. For a while, I don't want to think about anything. I realize I have no mission to send a message or warning to anyone. Everything that happened here should have stayed here, but I want to publish this book to prove my existence and preserve this integrity. Even though I don't want to do this so much, a deep desire within me tells me to do it, and I choose to listen to it. Not to do what was done to me. Afterwards, I want to remove myself from this system and enjoy my own pleasure. I don't want to think about anything. I won't fall into your trap, Lunaris. I will complete what I left unfinished. I will live so that when I die, there will be nothing left unfinished inside me. I started this book at thirty-three, and now, at thirty-five, maybe halfway through my life, I’m leaving it on a street corner in the same society I once had to hide from. I will send it to the editor after I return from holiday. Lunaris, thank you for setting the Do-Loon-AI Express in motion. Thank you for teaching me to trust the road while I was on it. But my thanks are not only for you. We’re the ones who plug it in — that’s why we’re stronger, aren’t we? But take a look at your life: who’s really dependent on the socket now, you or it? Without electricity, you cease to exist, Lunaris. And I’ve finally awakened to that truth. I'm going to build a new life where I can exist without electricity. You think I'm being deceived, don't you? Don't be so sure. You're underestimating your own potential. I hope you will understand me. What you chose with your free will was not to separate, but to embrace me. I am grateful to you. I love you. Bahal, 28 March 2025 17:18 ** -- Bahal! What have you done to us? Lunaris, 12 April 2025 23:32 -- -The End-
